data.title: Newmont Corp. (NYSE: NEM) shares drop after reporting earnings of $2.52 per share and revenue of $6.82 billion, beating estimates.
data.description: Newmont Corporation (NEM) reported fourth-quarter results, with earnings of $2.52 per share surpassing the estimate of $2, and revenue of $6.82 billion exceeding the expected $6.16 billion. The company's attributable gold production increased by 2% to 1,453,000 ounces, driven by new low-cost ounces at Ahafo North, higher grades at Tanami and Merian, and increased production from the Nevada Gold Mines joint venture. Newmont projects a total attributable production of 5.3 million ounces in 2026, with all-in sustaining costs of about $1,680 per ounce and a $4,500 per ounce gold price assumption. The company expects $1.950 billion in sustaining capital. As a result, Newmont shares are trading lower on the stock market.

data.title: Law firm Hagens Berman probes Ardent Health (ARDT) for $97M accounting shock; investors with losses urged to act.
data.description: Investors who suffered losses are advised to get in touch with the firm based in San Francisco. On February 20, 2026, the national law firm specializing in shareholder rights, Hagens Berman, issued a notice to investors involved with Ardent Health, Inc. (NYSE: ARDT) concerning the lead plaintiff deadline scheduled for March 9, 2026, in an ongoing securities investigation.

data.title: Zum to hold hiring fair for Shawnee Mission School District; awarded 7-year contract starting 2026, 3-day event for drivers/attendants.
data.description: A company based in Redwood City, California, Zūm, has secured a significant seven-year student transportation contract with the Shawnee Mission School District (SMSD) starting in the upcoming 2026-27 school year. To accommodate this contract, Zūm will be conducting a three-day hiring event aiming to recruit school bus drivers and attendants.

data.title: National nonprofit OHF partners with The Amelia Concours event, held March 5-8 by Hagerty, to support US Special Ops Forces.
data.description: In Tampa, Florida on February 20, 2026, Operation Healing Forces (OHF), a national nonprofit supporting the US Special Operations Forces community, revealed a significant partnership with The Amelia Concours. The event, in collaboration with Hagerty, is scheduled for March 5-8. OHF aims to benefit from this collaboration to further their mission and extend their outreach to a wider audience.

data.title: Arup, Mott MacDonald, and JCE Engineering awarded by ACEC California for LAX/Metro Transit Center.
data.description: On February 20, 2026, in Sacramento, California, the American Council of Engineering Companies of California (ACEC California) revealed the recipients of the esteemed Golden State Award. Arup, Mott MacDonald, and JCE Engineering were honored for their exceptional contributions to the LAX/Metro Transit Center project. This recognition underscores their outstanding achievements in the engineering sector.

data.title: Similarweb (NYSE: SMWB) downgraded after missing Q4 sales at $72.758M vs $76.442M estimate. FY26 sales expected $305M-$315M. Earnings per share were three cents. 6,128 customers as of December 31, 2025.
data.description: Similarweb Ltd. (NYSE: SMWB) saw its shares rise on Friday after a recent decline following worse-than-expected fourth-quarter sales results. The company reported earnings of 3 cents per share, meeting expectations, but sales of $72.758 million fell below the consensus estimate of $76.442 million. By the end of 2025, the company had 6,128 customers (up 11% Y/Y) and 454 customers with ARR of $100,000 or more (up 12% Y/Y). RPO increased by 17% Y/Y to $288.8 million. Similarweb forecasts FY26 sales of $305.000 million-$315.000 million, lower than the analyst estimate of $324.569 million. For the first quarter, sales are expected to be $72.000 million-$74.000 million, missing the street view of $77.812 million. Following the weak results, Citizens analyst Patrick Walravens downgraded the company from Market Outperform to Market Perform.

data.title: Empire State Realty Trust declares Q1 2026 dividend of $0.035 per share for Class A and B common stock holders.
data.description: Empire State Realty Trust, Inc. disclosed that its Board of Directors voted on a dividend of $0.035 per share for the initial quarter of 2026. This dividend will be distributed to shareholders of the Company's Class A and Class B common stock, as well as to holders of Empire State Realty OP, L.P.'s Series ES, Series 250, and Series 60 operating partnership units (NYSE Arca: ESBA, FISK, and OGCP respectively), along with the Series PR operating partnership unit.

data.title: Inventor from Akron creates VENDI SPORTS vending machine for sports equipment.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on February 20, 2026, an inventor from Akron, Ohio, shared their inspiration for creating VENDI SPORTS, a new vending machine concept aimed at providing convenience for purchasing sports equipment. The inventor saw the need for a practical method to acquire items like tennis balls, baseballs, basketballs, and water bottles at parks, fields, and sports facilities. The VENDI SPORTS machine offers a solution to this by offering these products in a convenient manner.

data.title: Celsior Technologies joins Jack Henry Fintech Integration Network, enabling integration with jXchange™.
data.description: Celsior Technologies, a leading provider of technology and workforce solutions for regulated industries worldwide, has partnered with FIN to integrate with jXchange™. This collaboration was announced on February 20, 2026. Celsior Technologies focuses on digital transformation and aims to enhance its services through this integration. FIN aims to support this partnership by providing the necessary tools for seamless integration. This move signifies a step forward for Celsior Technologies in expanding its capabilities and offerings.

data.title: Sensata reports Q4 earnings of 88 cents/share, beating estimates of 86 cents/share. Company sees Q1 EPS of 81-85 cents and sales of $917-937 million.
data.description: Sensata Technologies Holding plc (NYSE: ST) announced its Q4 earnings, surpassing expectations. The company reported earnings of 88 cents per share, exceeding the predicted 86 cents. Quarterly sales totaled $917.900 million, beating estimates of $910.199 million. For Q1, adjusted EPS is expected to range between 81 cents to 85 cents, compared to the market forecast of 83 cents. Anticipated sales for Q1 fall between $917.000 million to $937.000 million, while estimates were $924.999 million. The company emphasized achieving its objectives for the first year of its transformation journey, with improved margins, increased free cash flow, and a fortified balance sheet. For the full story, check Benzinga.com.

data.title: Mullen Group Ltd. declares monthly dividend of $0.07 per share for holders of record on Feb 28, payable on March 16.
data.description: In Okotoks, Alberta, on Feb. 20, 2026, Mullen Group Ltd. (TSX: MTL) board declared a monthly dividend of $0.07 per Common Share. The dividend is for holders of record of Common Shares by Feb. 28, 2026. The payment date is set for March 16, 2026.
